title: ESP Security Rule Commit Without Approved Change Record

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CIP-005-6 R1 Part 1.4.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-005-6 R1 Part 1.4 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.20: ESP Security Rule Commit Without Approved Change Record.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.20' running on index=network sourcetype=pan:config, index=itsm sourcetype=chg:firewall, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
spl:
index=network sourcetype=pan:config earliest=-7d
| where match(cmd,"(?i)commit")
| join type=left max=0 admin [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=chg:firewall earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(change_id) as change_id by submitter
    | rename submitter as admin
  ]
| where isnull(change_id)
| table _time, admin, object, cmd, change_id
description: We flag firewall commits affecting ESP rules when no linked change ticket exists, supporting controlled configuration of the security perimeter.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm, network and sourcetypes chg:firewall, chg:firewall`, pan:config. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Map Panorama admin IDs to ITSM submitter IDs. (2) Require change_id in commit messages where supported. (3) Weekly governance report. (4) Escalate repeat offenders. Indexes required: `index=itsm`, `index=network`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=chg:firewall`, `sourcetype=chg:firewall``, `sourcetype=pan:config`, `sourcetype=pan:config``.
visualization: Table (unapproved commits), Timeline, Bar chart (by admin)

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks

knownFalsePositives: ESP access from authorized engineering activities, vendor remote support windows, or scheduled audit observations.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:config`, `index=itsm` `sourcetype=chg:firewall`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map Panorama admin IDs to ITSM submitter IDs. (2) Require change_id in commit messages where supported. (3) Weekly governance report. (4) Escalate repeat offenders.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype=pan:config earliest=-7d
| where match(cmd,"(?i)commit")
| join type=left max=0 admin [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=chg:firewall earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(change_id) as change_id by submitter
    | rename submitter as admin
  ]
| where isnull(change_id)
| table _time, admin, object, cmd, change_id
```

Understanding this SPL

**ESP Security Rule Commit Without Approved Change Record (CIP-005-6 R1 Part 1.4)** — We flag firewall commits affecting ESP rules when no linked change ticket exists, supporting controlled configuration of the security perimeter.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:config`, `index=itsm` `sourcetype=chg:firewall`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: pan:config. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype=pan:config, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(cmd,"(?i)commit")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(change_id)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
Output fields: _time, admin, change_id, cmd, object.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP or perimeter firewall log (for example Palo Alto or FortiGate) for the same window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unapproved commits), Timeline, Bar chart (by admin)
grandmaExplanation: We flag firewall commits affecting ESP rules when no linked change ticket exists, supporting controlled configuration of the security perimeter. That is important for the big power grid, where proof needs to line up with what the rules expect.
